MeasurementToolMetadata.Instructions: There are 5 statement groups on this questionnaire. Read each group of statements carefully. Choose one statement in each group that best describes the way you have been feeling for the past week. Please note: The word 'occasionally' when used here means once or twice; 'often' means several times or more; and 'frequently' measns most of the time.

asrm_1.Description: Question 1 - In the past week, I:

Levels.1: did not feel happier or more cheerful than usual
Levels.2: occasionally felt happier or more cheerful than usual
Levels.3: often felt happier or more cheerful than usual
Levels.4: felt happier or more cheerful than usual most of the time
Levels.5: felt happier or more cheerful than usual all of the time

asrm_2.Description: Question 2 - In the past week, I:

Levels.1: did not feel more self-confident than usual
Levels.2: occasionally felt more self-confident than usual
Levels.3: often felt more self-confident than usual
Levels.4: frequently felt more self-confident than usual
Levels.5: felt extremely self-confident all of the time

asrm_3.Description: Question 3 - In the past week, I:

Levels.1: did not need less sleep than usual
Levels.2: occasionally needed less sleep than usual
Levels.3: often needed less sleep than usual
Levels.4: frequently needed less sleep than usual
Levels.5: could go all day and night without any sleep and still not feel tired

asrm_4.Description: Question 4 - In the past week, I:

Levels.1: did not talk more than usual
Levels.5: talked constantly and could not be interrupted

asrm_5.Description: Question 5 - In the past week, I:

Levels.1: have not been more active (either socially, at work, home or school) than usual
Levels.2: have occasionally been more active than usual
Levels.3: have often been more active than usual
Levels.4: have frequently been more active than usual
Levels.5: was constantly active or on the go all the time

asrm_total_score.Description: Global score comprised of the sum of all items. n/a if any individual item is n/a.
